zombie_page.title: Zombie Siege Chronicles
zombie_page.subtitle: A strategic Tower Defense featuring placement validation and a modular portal system.

about.title: The ultimate defense against the undead
desc: In a world devastated by the undead, survival depends on strategy. Zombie Siege Chronicles is a tower defense game where players must protect their shelter from relentless waves.
Developed in a collaborative environment, I was responsible for the core systemic mechanics, UI architecture, and high-level gameplay logic.
The project showcases deep integration between C++ backend systems and Blueprint visual feedback, ensuring a smooth and responsive experience.

gameplay.title: Strategy and Survival
items: • Defensive Arsenal: Deploy modular turrets and barriers. Some defenses, such as barbed wire, deal passive damage while blocking passage.
• Portal Strategy: A two-actor teleportation system that allows the player to instantly reposition across the battlefield.
• Dynamic Waves: Scalable difficulty through round-based stat boosts and the activation of multiple spawn points.
• Survivor Events: Random missions to rescue NPCs, rewarding the player with base health or extra resources.

tech.title: System Engineering
items: • Placement Validation: A collision-based system using specialized areas to verify if a defense can be built on a specific surface.
• C++ Portal Logic: Implementation of entry/exit vectors, cooldown timers, and visual state management for the teleportation network.
• Asynchronous Loading: Professional transitions via asynchronous loading screens to manage map assets without frame drops.
• UI Architecture: Complex structure for HUD and Shop, featuring real-time economy updates and modular inventory visualization.

systems.title: Optimization and Workflow
items: • Asset Standardization: Strict naming conventions and project hierarchy to facilitate collaboration within the development team.
• Hybrid Audio: Dynamic audio mixers for environmental immersion, integrated with positional SFX for combat actions.
• Spline Camera: Cinematic menu sequences using spline-based camera rigs for a dynamic 3D background.
• Collision Optimization: Custom Trace Channels to separate enemy navigation from player interaction.

gallery.architecture_desc: Full architectural map illustrating the relationships between Game Mode logic, enemy spawners, and structure hierarchy.

placement.title: Spatial Placement Validation
placement.desc: A robust system designed to enforce building restrictions by cross-checking item types against area-specific collision volumes.
placement.img1: Item Type Definition: A look at the Turret's internal properties using a lightweight 'uint8' variable to define building rules.
placement.img2: Validation Area Setup: The BP_TurretsArea trigger volume using a property selector and custom meshes to define building boundaries.

portals.title: Teleportation System
portals.desc: Technical analysis of the portal mechanics, featuring entry/exit validation, dynamic material states, and cooldown logic.
portals.img1: Portal actor analysis: features dynamic materials, invisible interaction planes for navigation, and a debug arrow for entry/exit vectors.
portals.img2: Invalid placement state: The portal turns red when attempting to build on the road, where structures are prohibited.
portals.img3: Valid placement state: Successful verification on sidewalks. The mesh turns blue, providing immediate visual feedback.

menu.title: Hybrid Cinematic Menu
menu.desc: An immersive main menu blending a real-time 3D environment with a complex UI and cinematic camera movements.
menu.img1: Spline Camera Rig: Implementation of a CineCamera attached to a custom spline rail for a smooth cinematic tracking shot.
menu.img2: Interactive Layering: Breakdown of the widget hierarchy showing the hit-test layering system for complex button interactions.

shop.title: Data-Driven Shop System
shop.desc: A modular UI architecture that populates content dynamically by reading from centralized C++ Data Tables.
shop.img1: Item Data Table: The Row Editor where prices, stats, and icons are managed for rapid gameplay balancing.
shop.img2: Shop UI Architecture: The main widget hierarchy using responsive containers to organize dynamically generated entries.
shop.img3: Modular Item Widget: A reusable widget that automatically updates its visuals based on the received row data.

gallery.waves_desc: Backend logic for wave escalation, managing enemy scaling and spawn point activation based on round progression.
